label: Core
description: African real estate — the physical asset itself. Initially residential and mixed-use properties in Nairobi, Tatu City, Mombasa, and Accra.

wedges.label: African Real Estate
wedges.description: The physical asset itself: African real estate, initially residential and mixed-use properties in Nairobi, Tatu City, Mombasa, and Accra.

label: Tokenized Assets
description: What makes the Core exist as a digital, ownable, transferable financial instrument — not a service rendered to the asset, but the mechanism that constitutes it as investable.

wedges.label: Asset-Level Token
wedges.description: One SPV and one token per individual real estate asset. Direct, traceable exposure tied to a single physical collateral.

wedges.label: Portfolio Mother-Token
wedges.description: One SPV and one aggregating token over the full portfolio. Designed to be the liquid layer; reduces idiosyncratic single-asset risk and is the layer expected to sustain secondary market activity as it matures.

label: Features / Services
description: The properties the investor directly experiences once the asset is tokenized. Features and services are one ring, not two — each feature is simultaneously a service delivered to the holder.

wedges.label: Fractionable
wedges.description: Institutional-scale assets broken into accessible ticket sizes for any allocator.

wedges.label: Liquidable / Routable
wedges.description: Exit is possible without waiting for a buyer to independently appear; routed through the put mechanism and the liquidity architecture.

wedges.label: Remortgageable
wedges.description: A first-order feature, equal in weight to the others. The token can be used as collateral to obtain liquidity without selling the position. Three channels: via DeFi (requires reliable price oracles), via external financial partners, or via the issuer itself (Gurumbé / the Liquidity SPV).

wedges.label: Programmable
wedges.description: Yield distribution, compliance checks, and governance can be automated and enforced in code rather than through manual intermediation.

wedges.label: Convenient
wedges.description: No notary, no independent due diligence burden, no traditional transaction overhead for the investor.

wedges.label: Info Rich
wedges.description: Asset performance data and tenant payment history are continuously captured. Feeds both the credit-scoring layer (tenant side) and the asset valuation layer (asset side): Loanable = Credit Score / Info Rich = Asset Performance.

wedges.label: Higher Yield
wedges.description: Yield differential versus comparable developed-market real estate — the basic economic hook for capital crossing into the asset class.

wedges.label: Yield from Day 0
wedges.description: Investor receives a partial yield ramp from the day of purchase, even during pre-construction, financed by Gurumbé / the Liquidity SPV against future rental income — not by the investor.

label: Enablers
description: The legal, operational, and data infrastructure that gives the Features real-world executable form. Four enablers channel inward into Features. The fifth — Liquidity SPV — is directionally distinct: it channels capital from the outer Liquidity ring into the system.

wedges.label: Oracles / Surveyors
wedges.description: Two sources feed the NAV and valuation layer: the asset's own track record (occupancy, payment, maintenance history) and external market intelligence — Estate Intel for continuous data, plus a RICS-certified local surveying firm (e.g. Citiscape, Kenya Valuers, Mwaka Musau in Nairobi) for the formal annual valuation.

wedges.label: Legal Structure SPV
wedges.description: The asset-level and mother-token SPV structures, incorporated in an appropriate African jurisdiction, holding title, custody agreements, and offtake contracts.

wedges.label: Operating Company
wedges.description: The entity that manages tenants, collects rent, coordinates with developers, and produces the operational data that Oracles consume and that Rent-from-Day-1 depends on. Distinct from the legal SPV and the token — this is the operational layer that makes the building function.

wedges.label: Credit Lines
wedges.description: The financing facilities — private debt notes, bank lines — that fund the lending book and the put book inside the Liquidity SPV. Distinct from the Liquidity ring itself: this is the funding source, not the liquidity-providing mechanism.

wedges.label: Liquidity SPV
wedges.description: The legal vehicle that channels the outer Liquidity ring (AMM, Anchor investors, Gurumbé) into operable form: issues private debt notes, holds the put book, runs the lending/remortgage book. Directionally distinct from the other four Enablers — it channels outside-in, from the Liquidity ring toward the rest of the system.

label: Liquidity
description: The outermost ring. Not parallel to the others — it is the gate that determines whether the entire system is investable to global capital. Liquidity is downstream of every other ring and upstream of the product’s institutional credibility.

wedges.label: AMM
wedges.description: Passive algorithmic liquidity pool for normal market conditions. Absorbs small and medium transactions once sufficient volume exists. Not assumed necessary at launch — access convenience and remortgageability substitute for it early on.

wedges.label: Anchor Investors
wedges.description: 3-5 institutional investors contractually committed to buy back tokens up to a defined volume at no less than -20/-30% of updated NAV, compensated with a premium or preferential access to future issuance.

wedges.label: Gurumbé (Last Resort)
wedges.description: The final backstop layer, funded from the Liquidity SPV, financed initially via private debt (target 8-12% coupon) and later via cheaper bank debt as the portfolio scales.
